XLocateLiveSignature

Loads the Xbox Live signature for the specified content, if the content exists. 

BOOL XLocateLiveSignature(
    HANDLE hSignature,
    PXCONTSIG_LIVE_SIGNATURE_INFO pxlsInfo,
    PBYTE pbLiveDigest
);

Parameters

hSignature
[in] Handle to content-signature data previously returned from the XLoadContentSignatures or XLoadContentSignaturesEx function.
pxlsInfo
[out] Pointer that, on successful return, points to an XCONTSIG_LIVE_SIGNATURE_INFO structure that contains the Xbox Live signature of the specified content.
pbLiveDigest
[out] Pointer that, on successful return, points to the digest of the signature data specified by the hSignature parameter. The length of the data returned is equal to the XCALCSIG_SIGNATURE_SIZE constant in the Xbox.h header file.

Return Values

The function returns TRUE if the Xbox Live signature specified by hSignature loaded successfully.

The function returns FALSE if a Xbox Live signature does not exist for the specified content or if the Xbox Live signature cannot be loaded. If the return value is FALSE, the contents of the pxlsInfo and pbLiveDigest parameters are undefined.

Remarks

The XLocateLiveSignature function loads the Xbox Live signature for a piece of content, if a signature exists. The Xbox Live signature provides an added layer of security to ensure that the installed content has not been tampered with; the signature also provides a way for the content to be revoked by the Xbox Live service.

Xbox Live signatures are present for all content that is downloaded through the Storage service. The Xbox Live signature data returned by XLocateLiveSignature can be verified with the Signature service using the XOnlineSignatureVerify function.

A title is required to load and verify the Xbox Live signatures of all content downloaded from the Storage service before use as long as the title is online. If XOnlineSignatureVerify does not return successfully, the title must not access any of the content in question. It is up to the title to determine whether the content should be removed from the hard disk in this case. Content can be removed from the hard disk by first closing the content handle by using the XCloseContentSignatures function and then calling the XRemoveContent function.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xbox.h, for include use Xtl.h.

Import Library: Use Xapilib.lib.

See Also

Storage Overview, Storage Functions, Content Signatures | XLoadContentSignatures, XCloseContentSignatures, XRemoveContent, XOnlineSignatureVerify, XCONTSIG_LIVE_SIGNATURE_INFO
